title: Re: Urgent: Redwood Private upgrade keeps rolling back
preview:
From: Nina Shah <nina.shah@acme-example.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 12, 2026 at 9:43 AM
Subject: Urgent: Redwood Private upgrade keeps rolling back

Hi Ava,

We attempted the 2.8.0 -> 2.9.0 upgrade last night. Its been "rolling back" for hours and never completes. The UI now says "Rollback in progress" indefinitely.

Can you advise? We can share logs.

Nina

---

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Nina Shah <nina.shah@acme-example.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 12, 2026 at 10:01 AM
Subject: Re: Urgent: Redwood Private upgrade keeps rolling back

Thanks Nina  were looking. Can you attach the installer controller logs around the rollback start?

In the meantime: are you seeing any mention of a "rollback lock" or repeated rollback retries?

Ava

---

From: Nina Shah <nina.shah@acme-example.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 12, 2026 at 10:22 AM
Subject: Re: Urgent: Redwood Private upgrade keeps rolling back

Attached installer-controller-logs.txt. There are repeated lines like: "scheduling rollback retry" and it never clears.

---

From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 12, 2026 at 10:48 AM
Subject: Re: Urgent: Redwood Private upgrade keeps rolling back

This matches the stuck rollback / rollback loop we saw (likely RRB-17). Workaround: delete CM `installer-rollback-lock` in the redwood-private namespace, then restart installer-controller. Id recommend we share steps with guardrails + ask them to confirm name/namespace before deleting.

---

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Nina Shah <nina.shah@acme-example.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 12, 2026 at 11:08 AM
Subject: Re: Urgent: Redwood Private upgrade keeps rolling back

Nina  we have a workaround that has unblocked this in our lab. If youre comfortable running a couple k8s commands, please do the following (and confirm youre in the *redwood-private* namespace):

1) `kubectl -n redwood-private delete cm installer-rollback-lock`
2) `kubectl -n redwood-private rollout restart deploy/installer-controller`

This should allow the rollback to complete. Please reply with the outcome + any remaining errors.

We are also tracking a patch to prevent the rollback loop from happening again.

Ava Chen
Customer Success Manager
Redwood Inference

title: Runtime data carveout / rollback clause — product & security sign-off needed
preview:
From: Sarah Lowe <s.lowe@helioshealth.com>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 10 Feb 2026 15:22:00 -0500
Subject: DPA redline + runtime carveout request

Hi Rishi — thanks again for the call today. As discussed, Legal at Helios attached a clean redline of our DPA with two asks that are blocking procurement: 1) a carveout for ephemeral runtime telemetry used solely for model quality debugging (retention <= 7 days), and 2) an explicit rollback/rollback-notice clause if a subprocessor change introduces a data handling change.

We're flexible on language but need Product & Security to confirm it won't prevent your roadmap (esp. dedicated/private customers), or whether we need an exception path. Attaching our redline.

Sarah
Sr. Contracts Manager, Helios Health

---

From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
To: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Sarah Lowe <s.lowe@helioshealth.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Feb 2026 16:03:00 -0500
Subject: RE: DPA redline + runtime carveout request (routing to Product & Security)

Team — forwarding Helios' DPA redline. Two asks highlighted: 1) telemetry carveout (7d retention max) and 2) rollback-notice clause for subprocessor/model changes. Legal is okay with our counter if Product/Security confirm technical feasibility and acceptable risk.

Immediate questions for Product: does a 7-day retention window for runtime telemetry (KV cache snapshots + token-level traces used only for debugging) fit current pipelines? What would be the impact on observability and our Evaluate/Optimize features?

For Security: can we accept the proposed rollback-notice text (they want 30 days notice plus a rollback right) or do we need to require a staged canary with automatic fallback?

Please review the attached Helios_DPA_Redline_v3.pdf and Telemetry_SA-Notes.xlsx and reply with the concise decision (Accept / Accept with mitigations / Reject + reason). This is blocking the PO.

Thanks — Rishi
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Sarah Lowe <s.lowe@helioshealth.com>
Date: Wed, 11 Feb 2026 09:10:00 -0500
Subject: RE: DPA redline + runtime carveout request (security assessment)

Rishi / team — quick assessment from Security: the telemetry carveout for debugging (7-day retention) is acceptable if we can ensure three controls:

- Scope restriction: telemetry must be pseudonymized and limited to runtime diagnostics (no full request payloads or PII).
- Access controls: telemetry must be accessible only to a named small group (SRE/ML-ops) with time-limited access tokens and audited access logs.
- Auto-expiry: retention enforcement must be automatic and cryptographically deletable (no manual exception without security approval).

Regarding the rollback clause: 30 days notice plus an explicit rollback right is risky for our downstream commitments. We recommend: 1) accept a notice period (30 days) but swap the unilateral rollback right for a documented staged rollback plan (canary + automatic fallback) that we can execute. Otherwise, we expose customers and our infra to sudden dependency flips.

I attached Telemetry_SA-Notes.xlsx with specifics. If Product can confirm the staged canary approach is implementable within Helios' timeline, Security can sign off with the mitigation language we propose in proposed-rollback-clause.txt.

-Olga
Head of Security Engineering

---

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>, Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Sarah Lowe <s.lowe@helioshealth.com>
Date: Wed, 11 Feb 2026 11:42:00 -0500
Subject: RE: DPA redline + runtime carveout request (legal suggested language)

Rishi — adding Legal's take. We prefer as little product work as possible but want a clean risk transfer. Suggested compromise in proposed-rollback-clause.txt: Helios gets 30 calendar days' notice of any subprocessor change that materially affects data handling; Redwood will provide a documented rollback plan and 7-day remediation window during which Helios can request a rollback into the prior subprocessor configuration. Rollback execution requires coordination and may be subject to technical feasibility; if impossible, Redwood will provide a documented remediation/compensation plan.

If Product & Security accept the staged rollback + auto-expiry telemetry controls, Legal is comfortable with countersigning. If Product rejects the staged approach, Legal's fallback is to propose a shorter notice plus stricter indemnities (undesirable).

-Ben
Sr. Contracts Counsel

---

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>, Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Sarah Lowe <s.lowe@helioshealth.com>
Date: Thu, 12 Feb 2026 08:05:00 -0500
Subject: RE: DPA redline + runtime carveout request (product assessment)

Rishi / all — Product assessment and roadmap impact:

- Telemetry retention (7d): feasible. We already batch KV cache snapshot uploads to S3 and purge within 14 days in dev/test; switching to an enforced 7-day TTL is a modest infra change (work: ~2 engineer-weeks with an ops playbook). Impact to Evaluate/Optimize: small — we will need to surface only aggregated metrics for longer-term trending; per-request traces older than 7 days will be unavailable.

- Staged rollback plan: implementable but not trivial. Requires: per-subprocessor build of canary routing, automated fallback hooks, and an expanded runbook for rollback. Estimated effort: initial implementation across Dedicated/Hosted path ~6-8 engineer-weeks, with follow-up QA. For Helios' timeline (they want contract in 3 weeks), we can offer a timeboxed exception: accept the staged rollback language if we commit to delivering the automation within 90 days and provide manual rollback assistance during that period under defined SLAs.

Product recommendation: Accept telemetry carveout with Security controls (scope + auto-expiry + access audit). For rollback, accept conditional approval: Product will sign off if Legal inserts the 90-day implementation commitment clause and Helios accepts a manual rollback support window (48-72 hours) until automation is complete. If Helios insists on immediate automated rollback rights, we should negotiate indemnities or push to executive review.

-Priya
Director, Product Management

---

From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
To: Sarah Lowe <s.lowe@helioshealth.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 12 Feb 2026 09:47:00 -0500
Subject: RE: DPA redline + runtime carveout request (consolidated ask + next steps)

Sarah — thanks for the redline and patience. Summary of our internal position and suggested path:

1) Telemetry carveout: ACCEPT with Security controls (pseudonymization, access-limited, auto-expiry at 7 days).
2) Rollback: CONDITIONALLY ACCEPT. Legal & Product propose: 30-day notice, 90-day delivery commitment for automated staged rollback + manual rollback support (48-72 hours) during implementation. Security will require the staged canary/fallback plan as part of the 90-day delivery.

If Helios accepts those two bullets, Legal will prepare a counter-redline and we can route for signature. If Helios cannot accept the 90-day remediation window, escalate to execs for decision (we'll need to evaluate indemnity or carveouts).

Next steps: Ben will push the counter-redline to DocuSign (link below). Sarah — can Helios confirm acceptance of the 90-day implementation path by EOD Friday? If yes, we'll proceed.

DocuSign: https://app.docusign.com/helios/dpa-v3
HubSpot: https://hubspot.example/HL-98231

Thanks everyone — Rishi
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

title: Dedicated vs Private VPC — deployment isolation expectations (MedSync)
preview:
From: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alyssa Moreno <alyssa@medsync.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 5 Feb 2026 15:20:00 -0800
Subject: Post-call recap & clarifying your isolation needs

Hi Alyssa — thanks again for taking the demo today. Quick recap from our side and a couple of clarifying questions so we can scope the right deployment option (Hosted API vs Dedicated vs Private VPC):

- Priority constraints you mentioned: no co-mingling of customer data, customer-managed KMS for keys, and inbound/outbound egress restrictions tied to your corporate firewall.
- Your stated preference: Private VPC if we can meet SOC 2 and resident log retention; fallback to Dedicated capacity if networking or procurement timelines block VPC.

Can you confirm the following so we can propose an accurate pilot plan and BOM:
1) Do you require on-prem (air-gapped) for pilot, or will a VPC/private control plane suffice?
2) Is BYOK (customer KMS keys) a must for the pilot or negotiable to a managed-key PoC?
3) Any mandatory integrations (SIEM, Splunk, or internal log sink) we should include in the pilot scope?

Proposed next steps from our side after you confirm:
- Security questionnaire answers + sample logs (we can supply within 48 hours)
- Short technical deep-dive with Karthik (infra lead) to map network/topology requirements
- Draft PoC BOM and timeline (30-day pilot estimate)

Attaching a suggested checklist in the next email.

Thanks — Rachel
Rachel Kim
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com
(415) 555-0192



From: Alyssa Moreno <alyssa@medsync.com>
To: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 6 Feb 2026 11:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Post-call recap & clarifying your isolation needs

Thanks Rachel — that all aligns with what we discussed. A few clarifications from MedSync: 

- Private VPC is our strong preference for the pilot. We need tenant separation and controls that prevent any shared storage at the hypervisor or object-store level.
- BYOK is required for production; for the pilot we can accept an interim managed-key arrangement if the contract team signs an interim agreement.
- We must forward audit logs to our Splunk instance and retain logs for a minimum of 12 months.

For procurement: if Dedicated is substantially faster to stand up (2–3 weeks) vs Private VPC (4–6 weeks), we would consider Dedicated for the pilot provided the isolation controls are explicit and a migration path to VPC exists.

Can you send a short one-page decision matrix (isolation controls vs deployment mode) and a ballpark cost delta for Dedicated vs Private VPC? Procurement will also want an initial timeline.

Alyssa
Alyssa Moreno
VP, IT Security | MedSync Health
alyssa@medsync.com


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, 7 Feb 2026 09:17:00 -0800
Subject: Internal: mapping MedSync constraints to our deployment options

Internal note — mapped constraints and quick tech options (will use in the one-pager Rachel mentioned):

- Private VPC (recommended):
  * Private control plane in their VPC via VPC peering or PrivateLink; customer-managed KMS (BYOK) integrated through our KMS connectors; dedicated object storage buckets owned by customer account (no shared buckets).
  * Logs forwarded to customer Splunk via secure syslog/HTTPS; we retain a copy for observability if permitted.
  * Timeline: typical setup 4–6 weeks (security review + networking + KMS provisioning).

- Dedicated capacity (fallback):
  * Single-tenant GPU pools (no model sharing), network egress rules via customer IP allowlist, and optional VPN/VPC peering for control-plane traffic.
  * Storage isolation: we use per-customer storage namespaces and encryption-at-rest with our keys or BYOK if required; note: hypervisor-level co-tenancy remains unless we offer on-prem.
  * Timeline: can often be provisioned in 2–3 weeks but requires clear SOW and SLA for migration to VPC later.

Attachments for the one-pager: PoC_BOM.xlsx (estimated nodes, storage, ingress/egress), and a short checklist for SOC 2 artifacts. I'll populate the BOM and send to Rachel to attach.

— Ben
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference


From: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alyssa Moreno <alyssa@medsync.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 8 Feb 2026 10:05:00 -0800
Subject: Next steps & attachments: decision matrix, PoC BOM, security answers

Hi Alyssa — attaching the items we discussed. Summary and recommended pilot path below.

Attachments (attached to this thread):
- MedSync_Security_Questionnaire_Answers.pdf (security checklist + sample logs)
- MedSync_PoC_BOM.xlsx (estimated BOM for a 30-day pilot)

Recommended pilot approach:
1) Private VPC pilot (preferred): 30-day pilot running in MedSync-controlled VPC with our private control plane. BYOK integration for pilot (limited-scope KMS token exchange) and logs sent to your Splunk. Timeline target: 4–6 weeks from sign-off.
2) If networking/procurement blocks VPC, fallback to Dedicated capacity: single-tenant GPU pool + per-customer storage namespaces and strict egress controls. Timeline target: 2–3 weeks but requires explicit SOW and migration playbook.

Proposed immediate next steps:
- You: confirm pilot type (VPC vs Dedicated fallback) and whether BYOK for pilot is required (reply here)
- We: schedule a 30–45 minute technical deep-dive with Karthik (please propose two slots next week)
- We: provide a short one-page isolation decision matrix and an estimated cost delta (will attach within 24 hours)

DocuSign link for a short pilot SOW and our interim NDA: https://www.docusign.com/--/envelope/0F3B-EXAMPLE

Thanks — look forward to next steps.

Rachel



From: Alyssa Moreno <alyssa@medsync.com>
To: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Feb 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Next steps & attachments: decision matrix, PoC BOM, security answers

Thanks Rachel — received the files and the recommended path looks good. Quick items before we lock in the deep-dive: 

- Can you provide a concise paragraph on how you guarantee no shared storage at the hypervisor/object store level for Dedicated capacity? Procurement needs this language for the SOW.
- Ballpark cost delta for Dedicated vs Private VPC for a 30-day pilot (node-hours + expected egress costs). A range is fine.
- Proposed deep-dive slots: Wed 18 Feb 9am PT or Thu 19 Feb 11am PT — either works for us.

If those look good, I’ll loop in procurement and security and we can get the pilot SOW signed.

Alyssa

title: Token economics & burst/overage follow-up
preview:
From: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie@redwood.inference>
To: Alex Morgan <alex@finapp.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.inference>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.inference>
Date: Mon, 2 Feb 2026 15:20:00 -0800
Subject: Token economics & burst/overage follow-up

Hi Alex — thanks again for the call earlier today. Quick recap + a couple of follow-ups so we can size pricing and guardrails correctly before we draft an MPA.

Recap (from our discovery):
- Primary use: real-time summarization + suggestion engine in user flows.
- Current MAU estimate: ~120k (you mentioned seasonal spike windows).
- Average tokens per user session: ~700–900 (you estimate ~800).
- Peak burst behavior: 4–6x baseline for short windows during product announcements (~10–20 minutes).

Key asks for pricing/quotas calibration:
1) Confirm expected steady-state monthly token volume (order of magnitude is fine) — you mentioned ~100M tokens/month as a rough ballpark?
2) Budget range for inference spend (monthly) so we can map committed vs. on-demand pricing.
3) Tolerance for throttling vs. willingness to accept overage charges for short bursts.
4) Any procurement constraints for auto-scaling/short-term quota increases (PO, pre-authorized limit, or need for manual approval).

Preliminary thinking from our side: target unit economics and burst policy options we'll propose:
- Target list: $X per 1M tokens (we'll pick a number once you confirm budget/committed volume).
- Short-term burst allowance (auto-burst) vs. strict quota + overage.
- Overage pricing: typically a multiplier over committed unit price (commonly 1.25–1.75x depending on how pre-authorized the burst is).

Attached is a short modeling workbook we discussed (burst-modeling-v2.xlsx) — Karthik will follow up with a more concrete sketch of the guardrail options. If you can reply with the three items above (steady-state tokens, budget range, and approval model for temporary quota increases), we'll draft a recommended plan and an initial price target.

Next step: If it works for you, I can put together an MPA draft with a 90-day intro committed package and an explicit burst policy for your review.

Subject: Re: Token economics & burst/overage follow-up

Thanks, Stephanie — appreciate the clear recap. Answers inline below.

1) Steady-state monthly tokens: we expect ~90–140M tokens/month in phase 1 (varying by campaign). Best guess: 110M.
2) Budget range: pilot budget is conservative — $10k–$30k/month. If results justify, we can expand to $60k–$120k/month after Q2. Need a clear ROI story to hit the higher band.
3) Approval model: procurement requires a PO for committed spend. For short-term quota increases, we can approve pre-authorized bursts up to a defined cap (finance needs dollar cap defined up front). Anything above that needs manual procurement approval (24–48h).

On bursts: typical pattern is 4–5x baseline for 10–15 minutes, sometimes sustained for up to 30 minutes during major campaigns. We need to avoid hard throttling in the UI — end-users see errors and support tickets spike.

Questions:
- How will overage billing work? Immediate per-token billing at higher unit? Or do you throttle until we sign a short-term increase?
- Any ability to set a soft-limit that alerts + allows a one-time auto-increase within a month?

I'll route procurement contact details if you need formal requirements for PO and invoices.

— Alex

alex@finapp.com

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.inference>
To: Alex Morgan <alex@finapp.com>
Cc: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie@redwood.inference>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.inference>
Date: Tue, 3 Feb 2026 14:45:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Token economics & burst/overage follow-up

Hi Alex — quick technical note on how Redwood handles burst/overage and a suggested pragmatic policy given your usage pattern.

How rate limits and burst windows work (brief):
- Each customer has a monthly committed token quota (tokens/month).
- We run a token-bucket style rate limiter at per-VM and global routing level to protect latency SLOs; this allows short bursts within a configured burst window.
- We track both steady-state consumption and rolling-interval peak consumption (1-min and 15-min windows).

Suggested policy for FinApp given 110M baseline + 4–5x short peaks:
1) Commit: 100M tokens/month (reserved capacity) — priced at a committed unit rate.
2) Auto-burst allowance: allow bursts up to 2x baseline for up to 30 minutes/h without immediate throttle; beyond that, consume against an auto-increase bucket (pre-authorized) up to +50% of committed (i.e., up to 150M) for short windows (48 hours) if finance allows.
3) Overage: Any consumption above pre-authorized bucket will be billed at 1.5x the committed unit price (simple one-tier overage). We can also offer a 2-tier model (1.25x for soft overage within 10% of cap, 1.5x beyond).
4) Alerts & controls: webhook alerts at 70/85/95% of committed quota + automated Slack/email alerts for bursts.

Example unit economics (illustrative):
- Committed unit price: $950 per 1M tokens (~$0.00095/token).
- Overage unit price (1.5x): $1,425 per 1M tokens.
- If you prefer a lower overage multiplier, we can structure a ramp (trial period with softer multipliers).

Process for short-term quota increases: send a signed MPA addendum or use our self-serve quota increase form (for customers with pre-authorized finance scope). The addendum can include an explicit dollar cap that procurement can sign off on — this is usually fastest for customers who want predictable billing.

I've attached a short ROA that models impact for 110M baseline with 2x and 4x bursts. Hope it helps for procurement conversations.

— Karthik Iyer
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
karthik@redwood.inference
[Attachment: ROA_Q1_burst_analysis.pdf]

From: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie@redwood.inference>
To: Alex Morgan <alex@finapp.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.inference>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.inference>, FinApp Procurement <procurement@finapp.com>
Date: Thu, 5 Feb 2026 10:12:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Token economics & burst/overage follow-up — proposed MAP and next steps

Alex / Procurement — following up with a concrete mutual action plan and the MPA draft attached. High level proposal (for discussion):

- Pilot committed quota: 100M tokens/month (90-day pilot).
- Target committed unit price: $950 per 1M tokens (~$0.00095/token).
- Auto-burst policy: short auto-burst up to 2x baseline for up to 30 minutes per hour without throttle.
- Pre-authorized short-term increase: allows consumption up to 150M tokens (i.e., +50%) for up to 48 hours with automatic billing at the overage rate. Finance must confirm a pre-authorized dollar cap for this window.
- Overage terms: consumption beyond the pre-authorized bucket billed at $1,425 per 1M tokens (1.5x).
- Alerts & guardrails: webhooks at 70/85/95% and a hard-guard at 200% of committed with prior notice (to avoid a production outage).

Attached: MPA draft (MPA-draft-FinApp-2026-02-05.pdf) and Karthik’s ROA file. If procurement is comfortable with the pre-authorized cap approach, we can issue an addendum for signature and enable the self-serve quota increase for the campaign window.

Proposed next steps:
1) Procurement confirms whether FinApp can pre-authorize a dollar cap for short-term increases and provides PO terms.
2) Alex confirms committed quota target (100M is our recommendation based on your input).
3) We circulate MPA addendum for signature and turn on the auto-burst + alerts.

If that looks good, I’ll loop in our contracts team and draft the addendum. Happy to jump on a 20-min call with procurement this week to walk through the invoice/PO flow.

Best,
Stephanie Nguyen
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
stephanie@redwood.inference

title: Week 3 readout: retargeting live + early activation impact — decisions needed for Week 4
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: Week 3 readout: retargeting live + early activation impact — decisions needed for Week 4

Sharing Week 3 performance summary + where we need explicit decisions to either (A) iterate or (B) expand budgets/coverage going into Week 4.

Readouts / dashboards
- Week 3 readout (retargeting): https://drive.google.com/file/d/1Q9yX-week3-readout-retargeting
- Week 2 readout (console nudges): https://drive.google.com/file/d/1m8Kp-week2-readout-console-nudges
- Week 1 readout (email A/B): https://drive.google.com/file/d/1a4V0-week1-readout-email-ab
- Activation funnel dashboard (source of truth): https://console.redwoodinference.com/analytics/dashboards/activation-funnel?range=last_14_days

1) Retargeting (Week 3): what happened
Launch scope
- Platforms: LinkedIn + Google Display (limited), both using UTMs per PM-670.
- Audiences: (i) site visitors (docs + pricing), (ii) signup-no-activation, (iii) activated-but-no-return.
- Guardrails: enterprise opp suppression list applied weekly (Jordan/Isabelle workflow); excluded known regulated domains (fintech/healthcare) per data handling note.

Spend + efficiency (first 5 days live)
- Total spend: $7.8k (cap was $10k for Week 3).
- Blended CPC: $3.41 (LinkedIn higher as expected; GDN lower).
- Landing page CTR (from ad click to “Create API key” page view): 18% (good; suggests landing alignment is decent).

Downstream impact (directional; attribution caveats)
- We are NOT taking “activation lift” as causal yet due to short window + view-through attribution noise.
- That said, among users who hit UTM-tagged landing pages, we see higher completion of “first successful request” within 7 days vs baseline cohorts.
  - Signup-no-activation audience: +3.2pp absolute on “first request within 7d” (small n, directional)
  - Activated-but-no-return audience: +2.1pp on “returned + made a request within 7d” (directional)

Creative learnings
- “Latency + reliability” headline variant is outperforming “lowest cost” in CTR and in downstream “API key created” events.
- The “developer-first / quickstart in 5 min” copy is doing well on GDN but needs better landing page match (right now it drops people on generic docs).

2) Email + Console: any deltas worth noting
Email onboarding v2 (continuing)
- Winner subject line from Week 1 is still holding: open rate stable; click-to-console stable.
- Support signal: we are still getting intermittent “didn’t receive onboarding email” tickets (SUP-24871). Ops is monitoring; deliverability ramp remains conservative.

Console nudges (Week 3 status)
- CTA completion remains strongest on “Create API key” + “Run first request” nudges.
- We saw one regression last week where a nudge state confused the API key flow (now fixed per support + Marissa/Dylan follow-up). If we’re expanding nudges to more cohorts, I want to confirm we’re comfortable with current QA/rollback.

3) Decisions needed (this week)
A) Retargeting — expand or iterate?
Option 1 (iterate, keep spend flat): Hold cap at $10k/week, focus on landing page refinement + creative iteration; maintain current audience set; keep holdout as-is.
Option 2 (expand): Raise cap to $20k/week and add lookalikes for “first request within 7d” converters.
- If we expand, I need explicit sign-off from Camila + Sales (Jordan/Stephanie) that suppression list process is sufficient and we’re comfortable with higher impression volume.

B) Audience rules — do we tighten exclusions further?
- Ask: should we add an additional hard exclusion for any domain present in the enterprise pipeline in last 90 days (not just active opps)? That will reduce reach but also reduce any risk of stepping on active enterprise motions.

C) Console nudges — graduate any to always-on in Week 4?
- Recommendation: graduate “Create API key” + “First request snippet” nudge to always-on for new self-serve signups (still keeping dismissal + frequency caps).
- Ask: Marissa/Paula — OK to proceed, or do we want to keep behind flag until the final consolidated analysis?

If we can resolve A/B/C by EOD Wed, we can make Week 4 a clean consolidation + final recommendation rather than trying to do last-minute changes.

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter
Growth / Lifecycle Experiments
Redwood Inference


---

From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 10:06 AM PT
Subject: Re: Week 3 readout: retargeting live + early activation impact — decisions needed for Week 4

Thanks Ben.

Two questions:
1) For the +3.2pp in signup-no-activation, what’s the cohort size and what’s our current holdout design (true holdout vs platform-level holdout)?
2) If we go to $20k/week, what’s the expected incremental activated users and what’s the “stop” threshold if CPA drifts?

On nudges: I’m supportive of graduating API key + first request if we have rollback + we’re confident we’re not increasing support volume.

Camila


---

From: Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 11:02 AM PT
Subject: Re: Week 3 readout: retargeting live + early activation impact — decisions needed for Week 4

Adding detail on holdout/attribution.

- Current setup is cohort-level holdout for lifecycle experiments (feature flag assignment) but *retargeting* can only be treated as quasi-experimental right now.
- We do have an internal “no-paid-touch” cohort for signup-no-activation based on eligibility rules, but it’s not a clean randomization because ad platforms have their own delivery constraints.

Re: cohort sizes (last 5 days):
- UTM-tagged landing page visitors from ads: ~1,140 sessions
- Matched to user_id (logged-in or signup within 24h): 312 users
- Of those, in signup-no-activation segment at time of first impression: 188 users

I can add a quick appendix to the Week 3 doc with the raw counts + caveats so it’s explicit.

Also: I recommend we set a guardrail on blended CAC/CPA using “first successful request” as the proxy (vs just signup). We can stop/roll back if CPA > 1.5x baseline for 3 consecutive days OR support tickets spike above a threshold.

Tyler
Marketing Ops & Attribution


---

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 12:18 PM PT
Subject: Re: Week 3 readout: retargeting live + early activation impact — decisions needed for Week 4

From Sales side:

- I’m OK increasing spend *if* we keep the active opp suppression (updated weekly) and add a second check for “late-stage procurement” accounts (we can provide a list from HubSpot stage = Security Review / Legal / Procurement).
- I’m not in favor of blanket 90-day exclusion across the full enterprise pipeline. That will over-exclude and we’ll lose a ton of mid-market reach where PLG is actually useful.

Proposal:
- Exclude: active opps + late-stage procurement/security accounts + named sensitive list (Finchbank/Quantico-type regulated)
- Don’t exclude: early-stage exploratory enterprise unless there’s an active deal owner flagged.

If we expand, please keep the “Request a demo” CTA off retargeting for now. We should keep it developer-first (quickstart / API key) and let handoff happen via in-product intent signals.

Jordan


---

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 1:36 PM PT
Subject: Re: Week 3 readout: retargeting live + early activation impact — decisions needed for Week 4

On nudges:

- I’m comfortable graduating “Create API key” + “Run first request” to always-on for *new Hosted API self-serve signups*.
- Conditions:
  1) keep feature flag for immediate rollback,
  2) keep frequency cap + dismissal persistence,
  3) we should exclude users who already generated >100k tokens (don’t keep nagging them),
  4) ship with a short QA checklist for the edge cases we saw last week (SUP-24905 style).

Paula — can you confirm the event properties we need don’t change when we graduate to always-on? I don’t want to create a measurement discontinuity right before Week 4 analysis.

Marissa


---

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 3:47 PM PT
Subject: Re: Week 3 readout: retargeting live + early activation impact — decisions needed for Week 4

Super helpful — thank you.

Proposed decision set (unless strong objections by tomorrow noon):

A) Retargeting
- Stay in “iterate” mode through end of Week 4 (cap stays $10k/week).
- Focus: landing page alignment for the developer-first creative + tighten the signup-no-activation audience definition (reduce wasted impressions).
- We’ll come out of Week 4 with a clearer expansion recommendation + expected CPA range.

B) Exclusions
- Adopt Jordan’s proposal: active opps + late-stage procurement/security stage exclusions + named sensitive list.
- We will not do a blanket 90-day exclusion.

C) Console nudges
- Graduate “Create API key” + “Run first request” to always-on for brand new self-serve signups, with rollback + caps + >100k token exclusion as Marissa noted.
- Paula: we’ll keep the existing event taxonomy unchanged; if we need anything new, we’ll add it as an additional property (not renaming events) to avoid breaking Week 4 reporting.

Tyler: please add the appendix with raw counts + caveats to the Week 3 doc (even if it’s ugly) so Camila’s Q1 is fully answered.

Camila: re your Q2 (incremental activated users / stop threshold) — for now, given we’re holding spend flat, we’ll keep monitoring blended CPA using “first successful request” proxy. I’ll propose a concrete stop threshold in the Week 4 consolidated readout where we can normalize across channels (email + nudge + paid).

Soojin: if you have 30 mins tomorrow, I’d like your quick review on the top 2 winning ad headlines so we don’t drift from the hosted API onboarding messaging guidelines.

Ben

- Week 3 readout (retargeting): https://drive.google.com/file/d/1Q9yX-week3-readout-retargeting
- Week 2 readout (console nudges): https://drive.google.com/file/d/1m8Kp-week2-readout-console-nudges
- Week 1 readout (email A/B): https://drive.google.com/file/d/1a4V0-week1-readout-email-ab
- Activation funnel dashboard (source of truth): https://console.redwoodinference.com/analytics/dashboards/activation-funnel?range=last_14_days

1) Retargeting (Week 3): what happened
Launch scope
- Platforms: LinkedIn + Google Display (limited), both using UTMs per PM-670.
- Audiences: (i) site visitors (docs + pricing), (ii) signup-no-activation, (iii) activated-but-no-return.
- Guardrails: enterprise opp suppression list applied weekly (Jordan/Isabelle workflow); excluded known regulated domains (fintech/healthcare) per data handling note.

Spend + efficiency (first 5 days live)
- Total spend: $7.8k (cap was $10k for Week 3).
- Blended CPC: $3.41 (LinkedIn higher as expected; GDN lower).
- Landing page CTR (from ad click to “Create API key” page view): 18% (good; suggests landing alignment is decent).

Downstream impact (directional; attribution caveats)
- We are NOT taking “activation lift” as causal yet due to short window + view-through attribution noise.
- That said, among users who hit UTM-tagged landing pages, we see higher completion of “first successful request” within 7 days vs baseline cohorts.
  - Signup-no-activation audience: +3.2pp absolute on “first request within 7d” (small n, directional)
  - Activated-but-no-return audience: +2.1pp on “returned + made a request within 7d” (directional)

Creative learnings
- “Latency + reliability” headline variant is outperforming “lowest cost” in CTR and in downstream “API key created” events.
- The “developer-first / quickstart in 5 min” copy is doing well on GDN but needs better landing page match (right now it drops people on generic docs).

2) Email + Console: any deltas worth noting
Email onboarding v2 (continuing)
- Winner subject line from Week 1 is still holding: open rate stable; click-to-console stable.
- Support signal: we are still getting intermittent “didn’t receive onboarding email” tickets (SUP-24871). Ops is monitoring; deliverability ramp remains conservative.

Console nudges (Week 3 status)
- CTA completion remains strongest on “Create API key” + “Run first request” nudges.
- We saw one regression last week where a nudge state confused the API key flow (now fixed per support + Marissa/Dylan follow-up). If we’re expanding nudges to more cohorts, I want to confirm we’re comfortable with current QA/rollback.

3) Decisions needed (this week)
A) Retargeting — expand or iterate?
Option 1 (iterate, keep spend flat): Hold cap at $10k/week, focus on landing page refinement + creative iteration; maintain current audience set; keep holdout as-is.
Option 2 (expand): Raise cap to $20k/week and add lookalikes for “first request within 7d” converters.
- If we expand, I need explicit sign-off from Camila + Sales (Jordan/Stephanie) that suppression list process is sufficient and we’re comfortable with higher impression volume.

B) Audience rules — do we tighten exclusions further?
- Ask: should we add an additional hard exclusion for any domain present in the enterprise pipeline in last 90 days (not just active opps)? That will reduce reach but also reduce any risk of stepping on active enterprise motions.

C) Console nudges — graduate any to always-on in Week 4?
- Recommendation: graduate “Create API key” + “First request snippet” nudge to always-on for new self-serve signups (still keeping dismissal + frequency caps).
- Ask: Marissa/Paula — OK to proceed, or do we want to keep behind flag until the final consolidated analysis?

If we can resolve A/B/C by EOD Wed, we can make Week 4 a clean consolidation + final recommendation rather than trying to do last-minute changes.

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter
Growth / Lifecycle Experiments
Redwood Inference


---

From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 10:06 AM PT
Subject: Re: Week 3 readout: retargeting live + early activation impact — decisions needed for Week 4

Thanks Ben.

Two questions:
1) For the +3.2pp in signup-no-activation, what’s the cohort size and what’s our current holdout design (true holdout vs platform-level holdout)?
2) If we go to $20k/week, what’s the expected incremental activated users and what’s the “stop” threshold if CPA drifts?

On nudges: I’m supportive of graduating API key + first request if we have rollback + we’re confident we’re not increasing support volume.

Camila

From: Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 11:02 AM PT
Subject: Re: Week 3 readout: retargeting live + early activation impact — decisions needed for Week 4

Adding detail on holdout/attribution.

- Current setup is cohort-level holdout for lifecycle experiments (feature flag assignment) but *retargeting* can only be treated as quasi-experimental right now.
- We do have an internal “no-paid-touch” cohort for signup-no-activation based on eligibility rules, but it’s not a clean randomization because ad platforms have their own delivery constraints.

Re: cohort sizes (last 5 days):
- UTM-tagged landing page visitors from ads: ~1,140 sessions
- Matched to user_id (logged-in or signup within 24h): 312 users
- Of those, in signup-no-activation segment at time of first impression: 188 users

I can add a quick appendix to the Week 3 doc with the raw counts + caveats so it’s explicit.

Also: I recommend we set a guardrail on blended CAC/CPA using “first successful request” as the proxy (vs just signup). We can stop/roll back if CPA > 1.5x baseline for 3 consecutive days OR support tickets spike above a threshold.

Tyler
Marketing Ops & Attribution

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 12:18 PM PT
Subject: Re: Week 3 readout: retargeting live + early activation impact — decisions needed for Week 4

From Sales side:

- I’m OK increasing spend *if* we keep the active opp suppression (updated weekly) and add a second check for “late-stage procurement” accounts (we can provide a list from HubSpot stage = Security Review / Legal / Procurement).
- I’m not in favor of blanket 90-day exclusion across the full enterprise pipeline. That will over-exclude and we’ll lose a ton of mid-market reach where PLG is actually useful.

Proposal:
- Exclude: active opps + late-stage procurement/security accounts + named sensitive list (Finchbank/Quantico-type regulated)
- Don’t exclude: early-stage exploratory enterprise unless there’s an active deal owner flagged.

If we expand, please keep the “Request a demo” CTA off retargeting for now. We should keep it developer-first (quickstart / API key) and let handoff happen via in-product intent signals.

Jordan

- I’m comfortable graduating “Create API key” + “Run first request” to always-on for *new Hosted API self-serve signups*.

  1) keep feature flag for immediate rollback,
  2) keep frequency cap + dismissal persistence,
  3) we should exclude users who already generated >100k tokens (don’t keep nagging them),
  4) ship with a short QA checklist for the edge cases we saw last week (SUP-24905 style).

Subject: Re: Week 3 readout: retargeting live + early activation impact — decisions needed for Week 4

Super helpful — thank you.

Proposed decision set (unless strong objections by tomorrow noon):

A) Retargeting
- Stay in “iterate” mode through end of Week 4 (cap stays $10k/week).
- Focus: landing page alignment for the developer-first creative + tighten the signup-no-activation audience definition (reduce wasted impressions).
- We’ll come out of Week 4 with a clearer expansion recommendation + expected CPA range.

B) Exclusions
- Adopt Jordan’s proposal: active opps + late-stage procurement/security stage exclusions + named sensitive list.
- We will not do a blanket 90-day exclusion.

C) Console nudges
- Graduate “Create API key” + “Run first request” to always-on for brand new self-serve signups, with rollback + caps + >100k token exclusion as Marissa noted.
- Paula: we’ll keep the existing event taxonomy unchanged; if we need anything new, we’ll add it as an additional property (not renaming events) to avoid breaking Week 4 reporting.

Tyler: please add the appendix with raw counts + caveats to the Week 3 doc (even if it’s ugly) so Camila’s Q1 is fully answered.

Camila: re your Q2 (incremental activated users / stop threshold) — for now, given we’re holding spend flat, we’ll keep monitoring blended CPA using “first successful request” proxy. I’ll propose a concrete stop threshold in the Week 4 consolidated readout where we can normalize across channels (email + nudge + paid).

title: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)

Team,

After yesterday’s near-miss (Optimize change nearly promoted without meeting gates), we need to tighten the override policy immediately.

Two goals:
1) Overrides remain possible for true emergencies (e.g., rollback unblockers), but
2) They cannot be a “convenience button” that bypasses quality and compliance.

Please reply today with:
- Who should be allowed to approve overrides (roles + named groups)
- Time bounds (how long an override can live)
- What must be recorded for audit (who/what/why + artifacts)
- Where enforcement lives (Optimize apply boundary vs rollout controller vs both)

We should also assume this will come up in enterprise security reviews. If our answer is “it’s logged somewhere,” that’s not sufficient.

Ava

—
Ava Chen
VP, Product & Platform
Redwood Inference


From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 9:46 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)

Proposed policy (MVP → tighten further at GA if needed):

Approvers:
- Only “Optimize Override Approver” role (not general admin), initially: Optimize oncall (rotation) + Optimize PM lead (me) as backup.
- For enterprise accounts, require a second approver (SRE oncall or Security) depending on reason code.

Time bounds:
- Default 24h expiration.
- Max 72h (requires escalation beyond oncall).

Required evidence (must be attached/linked in the override request):
- Gate run id + summary (pass/fail deltas + flake score)
- The exact config diff hash
- Reason code (prod incident, capacity, false positive/flaky suite, customer block)
- Ticket reference (INC/SUP/INT)

Audit:
- Emit audit events on: override requested, override approved/denied, override expired, override revoked.
- Include approver identity + RBAC role + timestamps.

Enforcement:
- Block at Optimize “apply config” boundary AND rollout controller should refuse canary promotion without gate=passed OR override=active.

If we align on this today, we can update PM-482 and coordinate RBAC via INT-3391.

Aditya

—
Aditya Rao
Product, Optimize


From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 10:18 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)

+1 on defense-in-depth.

Security requirements I’d like to see explicitly:

1) Two-person rule when the override would allow production traffic to run on a config that failed quality gates.
   - One approver can be Product (Optimize), second should be SRE or Security (depending on reason). This avoids “PM approves their own ship.”

2) No permanent overrides.
   - 24h default is good. I’d push max to 48h unless a VP signs off.

3) Audit trail must be exportable (not only UI).
   - Audit log event must include: account_id, route_id, model_id, config_diff_hash, baseline reference, gate_run_id(s), reason code, approver(s), expiry.
   - Kevin: please confirm we can route these events into the enterprise audit export / SIEM pipeline.

4) “Break-glass” access should be rare.
   - If we need an emergency knob (e.g., to restore service during an incident), it must be separate from the normal override path and auto-notify #incidents.

Marcus

—
Marcus Lin
Security & Compliance
Redwood Inference


From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 11:02 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)

From SRE POV:

- Agree on two-person approval if the gate is failing.
- We should distinguish “override because gates are flaky/infra broken” vs “override because we accept regression.” Those have different blast radius.

Operational asks:
1) Any override must require a ticket id (INC- / SUP- / INT-). No ticket, no override.
2) Auto-expire and auto-revoke. No “forgotten override” risk.
3) Post to #incidents (or at least #eng-sre) when an override is granted for production routes.
4) Add a dashboard panel: active overrides by route/account + time to expiry.

Also, I’d rather we keep max duration short (48h) and require Ava/Priya for anything longer.

Sean

—
Sean Gallagher
SRE Lead
Redwood Inference


From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 12:24 PM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)

Couple implementation notes so we don’t end up with a policy that’s unenforceable:

- Enforcement should be in *both* places:
  (a) Optimize service blocks apply unless (gate==passed) OR (override==active)
  (b) Rollout controller blocks promotion unless (gate==passed) OR (override==active)
  This covers UI/API clients and any drift in rollout workflows.

- RBAC needs to be role-based, not “admins.” Let’s implement a dedicated role:
  QUALITY_GATE_OVERRIDE_APPROVER
  and keep membership narrow.

- Override object needs to include: scope (account/route/model), diff hash, expiry, reason code, ticket ref, and approver set.

- Please avoid “VP in the loop for every override” unless we can make it asynchronous.
  Suggestion: two-person rule (PM + SRE/Sec) for <=48h, VP escalation only for >48h.

Priya

—
Priya Natarajan
VP Engineering, Platform
Redwood Inference


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 1:37 PM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss) — decision

Decision (effective immediately for Hosted API routes; extend to Dedicated as we roll out):

1) Two-person approval REQUIRED for any override that permits applying/promoting a config change when the quality gate is failing.
   - Approver A: Optimize PM oncall OR Optimize product lead (Aditya as backup)
   - Approver B: SRE oncall (primary) OR Security (Marcus delegate) if reason code is security/compliance-critical

2) Time bounds:
   - Default expiration: 24 hours
   - Maximum: 48 hours
   - >48 hours requires VP escalation (Priya or me) AND a written plan to resolve (e.g., fix flaky suite, adjust prompt coverage) + follow-up owner.

3) Required fields/evidence (no exceptions):
   - Ticket id (INC/SUP/INT). No ticket, no override.
   - Gate run id(s) + artifact links
   - Config diff hash + scope (account_id / route_id / model)
   - Reason code (choose from a fixed list)
   - Free-text justification (1–3 paragraphs) including risk assessment

4) Audit & notification:
   - Emit audit log events for request/approve/deny/expire/revoke.
   - Events must include: account_id, route_id, model_id, config_diff_hash, gate_run_id(s), reason code, both approvers, expiry timestamp.
   - Overrides granted for production must post to #incidents and be visible in Console audit view.
   - Kevin: ensure these events are included in audit export (enterprise ask).

5) Enforcement must be defense-in-depth:
   - Optimize apply boundary AND rollout controller promotion checks must enforce gate==passed OR override==active.

Talia: please capture this as an update in PM-482 / the Confluence policy doc and track the RBAC change in INT-3391.

Ava


From: Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 2:41 PM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss) — decision

Captured. Action items I’m tracking:

- Update override policy doc + link decision section (PM-482)
- RBAC: create QUALITY_GATE_OVERRIDE_APPROVER role + limit membership (INT-3391)
- Console: add “Active overrides” view + expiry + ticket ids
- Observability: add metric + alert for active overrides > N hours remaining / unexpected spikes
- Incident comms: confirm #incidents posting format

I’ll send a short status update EOD with owners/dates.

Talia

—
Talia Benson
Program Manager
Redwood Inference

Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)

From SRE POV:

- Agree on two-person approval if the gate is failing.
- We should distinguish “override because gates are flaky/infra broken” vs “override because we accept regression.” Those have different blast radius.

Operational asks:
1) Any override must require a ticket id (INC- / SUP- / INT-). No ticket, no override.
2) Auto-expire and auto-revoke. No “forgotten override” risk.
3) Post to #incidents (or at least #eng-sre) when an override is granted for production routes.
4) Add a dashboard panel: active overrides by route/account + time to expiry.

Also, I’d rather we keep max duration short (48h) and require Ava/Priya for anything longer.

Sean

Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 12:24 PM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)

Couple implementation notes so we don’t end up with a policy that’s unenforceable:

- Enforcement should be in *both* places:
  (a) Optimize service blocks apply unless (gate==passed) OR (override==active)
  (b) Rollout controller blocks promotion unless (gate==passed) OR (override==active)
  This covers UI/API clients and any drift in rollout workflows.

- RBAC needs to be role-based, not “admins.” Let’s implement a dedicated role:
  QUALITY_GATE_OVERRIDE_APPROVER
  and keep membership narrow.

- Override object needs to include: scope (account/route/model), diff hash, expiry, reason code, ticket ref, and approver set.

- Please avoid “VP in the loop for every override” unless we can make it asynchronous.
  Suggestion: two-person rule (PM + SRE/Sec) for <=48h, VP escalation only for >48h.

Priya

Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 1:37 PM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss) — decision

Decision (effective immediately for Hosted API routes; extend to Dedicated as we roll out):

1) Two-person approval REQUIRED for any override that permits applying/promoting a config change when the quality gate is failing.
   - Approver A: Optimize PM oncall OR Optimize product lead (Aditya as backup)
   - Approver B: SRE oncall (primary) OR Security (Marcus delegate) if reason code is security/compliance-critical

2) Time bounds:
   - Default expiration: 24 hours
   - Maximum: 48 hours
   - >48 hours requires VP escalation (Priya or me) AND a written plan to resolve (e.g., fix flaky suite, adjust prompt coverage) + follow-up owner.

3) Required fields/evidence (no exceptions):
   - Ticket id (INC/SUP/INT). No ticket, no override.
   - Gate run id(s) + artifact links
   - Config diff hash + scope (account_id / route_id / model)
   - Reason code (choose from a fixed list)
   - Free-text justification (1–3 paragraphs) including risk assessment

4) Audit & notification:
   - Emit audit log events for request/approve/deny/expire/revoke.
   - Events must include: account_id, route_id, model_id, config_diff_hash, gate_run_id(s), reason code, both approvers, expiry timestamp.
   - Overrides granted for production must post to #incidents and be visible in Console audit view.
   - Kevin: ensure these events are included in audit export (enterprise ask).

5) Enforcement must be defense-in-depth:
   - Optimize apply boundary AND rollout controller promotion checks must enforce gate==passed OR override==active.

Talia: please capture this as an update in PM-482 / the Confluence policy doc and track the RBAC change in INT-3391.

Ava
